basics.name: Númi Sveinsson Cepero
basics.label: Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering | Computational Modeling & Medical Imaging
basics.summary: Computational modeling and medical imaging researcher specializing in biofluid dynamics and deep learning for cardiovascular applications. Experienced in developing machine learning frameworks for vessel segmentation and automatic vascular model construction. Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of California, Berkeley (2025). Currently a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Oden Institute, The University of Texas at Austin.

education.details: Major: Biomechanics | Minor 1: Machine Learning | Minor 2: Scientific Computing

education.summary: Graduated with the highest GPA in program history.

research.name: The University of Texas at Austin — Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences
research.summary: Research focuses on integrating artificial intelligence, medical imaging, and computational modeling to study physiology and advance medicine. Expanding research to explore clinically driven applications of AI and simulation, with the goal of developing digital tools that bridge computational models and patient care.

research.name: University of California, Berkeley — Mechanical Engineering Department
research.summary: Earned Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering. Work with Prof. Shawn Shadden combined biomechanics, machine learning, and image-based modeling to improve vascular segmentation and simulation. Doctoral projects introduced open-source tools for automated vascular reconstruction and simulation-ready mesh generation from medical images.
highlights: SeqSeg: Open-source tool for automated vascular reconstruction
MeshGrow: Open-source tool for simulation-ready mesh generation from medical images

research.name: University of Iceland — Department of Medicine

work.name: University of California, Berkeley — Mechanical Engineering Department
work.summary: Developed deep learning–based methods for automatic vascular model construction and trajectory-oriented vessel tracking in medical imaging. Integrated physics-based modeling and machine learning to enable patient-specific cardiovascular simulations.
highlights: Developed SeqSeg: a deep learning framework for coronary artery segmentation
Created MeshGrow: an automated pipeline for unified cardiac and vascular mesh generation
Contributed to SimVascular open-source cardiovascular modeling software
Mentored undergraduate and MEng teams in medical image analysis projects

work.name: Kerecis
work.summary: Worked on material property analysis for medical biomaterials.
highlights: Collaborated on R&D for regenerative medical materials.

work.name: University of Iceland — Department of Medicine
work.summary: Developed computational models for new lung experimental technologies.

publications.name: SeqSeg: Learning Local Segments for Automatic Vascular Model Construction
publications.summary: N. Sveinsson Cepero and S. C. Shadden. Presents a deep learning method for segmenting and reconstructing vascular models from medical imaging data.

publications.name: Integrated Framework for Unified Cardiac and Vascular Mesh Construction from Medical Images
publications.publisher: Functional Imaging and Modeling of the Heart (FIMH 2025)
publications.summary: N. Sveinsson Cepero, A. Narayanan, and S. C. Shadden. Introduces MeshGrow, a unified framework for simulation-ready cardiovascular mesh generation.

publications.name: Automatic Vascular Model Construction from Medical Imaging Using Deep Learning
publications.summary: Ph.D. Dissertation. Explores deep learning for automated vascular model construction.

service.position: Reviewer for MICCAI (Medical Image Computing and Computer Assisted Intervention) Conference submissions
Co-organizer and instructor for SimVascular tutorials and workshops at international conferences
Volunteer contributor to the SimVascular open-source community
service.summary: Academic service — software development, documentation, and user support
Academic service — advising on curriculum and research initiatives

service.organization: SINE – Association of Icelandic Students Abroad
SINE – Association of Icelandic Students Abroad

service.position: Board Member, School of Engineering and Natural Sciences

awards.title: UC Berkeley Engineering Ph.D. Commencement Speech
UC Berkeley Mechanical Engineering Department Spring Scholarship
UC Berkeley Mechanical Engineering Department Summer Fellowship
UC Berkeley Graduate Division Block Grant
University of Iceland Student Achievement & Incentive Fund

projects.name: SeqSeg
projects.summary: Deep learning–based framework for automated segmentation of coronary arteries from CT images.

projects.name: MeshGrow
projects.summary: Framework for unified cardiac and vascular mesh construction from medical images.

projects.name: SimVascular
projects.summary: Open-source cardiovascular modeling software. Contributed features and documentation.

projects.name: MIROS
projects.summary: Toolkit linking patient-specific medical imaging data to reduced-order hemodynamic simulations.

projects.name: Vascular Model Repository
projects.summary: Online platform for sharing and visualizing vascular geometries and simulation data.
